title: The Secret of Chimneys
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: First appearance of Superintendent Battle. Anthony Cade is revealed to be Prince Nicholas of Herzoslovakia.

persons.name: Anthony Cade
persons.notes: Revealed to be Prince Nicholas of Herzoslovakia

persons.name: Superintendent Battle
persons.notes: First appearance

persons.name: Prince Michael
persons.notes: Heir to the Herzoslovakian throne

persons.name: Miss Brun
persons.notes: Actually the last queen consort of Herzoslovakia, believed dead

persons.name: Lord Caterham
persons.notes: Owner of Chimneys

persons.name: Bundle Brent
persons.notes: Lord Caterham's daughter

persons.name: King Victor
persons.notes: Legendary jewel thief seeking the Herzoslovakian crown jewels

deaths.cause_detail: Shot by Miss Brun during a confrontation; she recognised him and shot him to prevent him claiming the throne and exposing her identity
deaths.motive_detail: Prince Michael recognised Miss Brun as the former queen consort and she killed him to protect her secret identity
deaths.notes: Miss Brun was the last queen consort of Herzoslovakia, believed murdered in the revolution; she had been hiding in plain sight

title: The Seven Dials Mystery
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Sequel to The Secret of Chimneys. Features Bundle Brent as the amateur sleuth alongside Superintendent Battle.

persons.name: Bundle Brent
persons.notes: Lady Eileen 'Bundle' Brent, Lord Caterham's daughter

persons.name: Gerald Wade
persons.notes: Known for oversleeping; found dead after the alarm clock prank

persons.name: Jimmy Thesiger
persons.notes: Revealed to be the killer

persons.name: Ronny Devereux
persons.notes: Shot after he began investigating the case

persons.name: Lord Caterham
persons.notes: Owner of Chimneys

persons.name: Sir Oswald Coote
persons.notes: Renting Chimneys for the season

persons.name: Loraine Wade
persons.notes: Gerald Wade's sister

deaths.cause_detail: Found dead in his room after the alarm clock prank; death initially ruled accidental overdose of sleeping drug but was in fact murder
deaths.motive_detail: Wade had discovered the Seven Dials conspiracy and had to be silenced
deaths.notes: Initial death that sets the plot in motion; Jimmy Thesiger is the surprising killer

deaths.cause_detail: Shot while investigating the Seven Dials case; jumped in front of Bundle's car before dying, implicating Jimmy Thesiger
deaths.motive_detail: Ronny Devereux was getting too close to uncovering the conspiracy
deaths.notes: His dying words 'Seven Dials... Tell... Jimmy Thesiger' drive Bundle to investigate

title: The Sittaford Mystery
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Also published as Murder at Hazelmoor in the US. Features Emily Trefusis and Inspector Narracott.

persons.name: Emily Trefusis
persons.notes: Investigates to exonerate her fiance

persons.name: Captain Trevelyan
persons.notes: Owner of Sittaford House; murdered with a sandbag

persons.name: Major Burnaby
persons.notes: Trevelyan's oldest friend; used skis to travel to the murder scene quickly

persons.name: Mrs Willett
persons.notes: Renting Sittaford House; hosted the table-turning seance

persons.name: Violet Willett
persons.notes: Mrs Willett's daughter

persons.name: Jim Pearson
persons.notes: Emily's fiance; initially suspected of the murder

deaths.cause_detail: Bludgeoned to death with a sandbag in his house in Exhampton; Major Burnaby used skis to quickly cover the six miles from the seance to the murder scene and back
deaths.motive_detail: Major Burnaby stood to inherit money from Trevelyan, whose competition winnings were in Burnaby's name
deaths.notes: The séance's spirit 'message' that Trevelyan was dead was manipulated; Burnaby used skis to create a false alibi based on walking time

title: Why Didn't They Ask Evans?
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Also published as The Boomerang Clue in the US. Standalone novel featuring Bobby Jones and Lady Frances Derwent.

persons.name: Bobby Jones
persons.notes: Fourth son of the Vicar of Marchbolt

persons.name: Lady Frances Derwent
persons.notes: Known as Frankie; Bobby's investigating partner

persons.name: Alan Carstairs
persons.notes: Found dying on the golf course; his last words are the novel's title question

persons.name: Roger Bassington-ffrench
persons.notes: Pushed Carstairs off a cliff; impersonated John Savage to forge a will

persons.name: Moira Nicholson
persons.notes: Also known as Rose Templeton; Roger's accomplice

persons.name: John Savage
persons.notes: Murdered by Moira Nicholson with chloral hydrate after being convinced he was dying

persons.name: Gladys Evans
persons.notes: The Evans of the title; would have recognised the fake Savage signing the will

deaths.cause_detail: Pushed off a cliff at Marchbolt golf course by Roger Bassington-ffrench; found dying by Bobby Jones and uttered the cryptic question 'Why didn't they ask Evans?'
deaths.motive_detail: Carstairs was investigating John Savage's death and had discovered the fraud; he had to be silenced
deaths.notes: His dying words set the entire plot in motion

deaths.cause_detail: Murdered by Moira Nicholson who convinced him he was dying of cancer, then killed him with chloral hydrate to inherit his fortune via a forged will
deaths.motive_detail: Moira and Roger schemed to obtain Savage's fortune through a forged will
deaths.notes: The backstory murder that motivates the entire conspiracy

title: Sparkling Cyanide
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Also published as Remembered Death in the US. Last appearance of Colonel Race. Expands Christie's short story Yellow Iris.

persons.name: Colonel Race
persons.notes: Last appearance in Christie's work

persons.name: Rosemary Barton
persons.notes: Died at restaurant dinner; death initially ruled suicide

persons.name: George Barton
persons.notes: Rosemary's widower; poisoned at the anniversary dinner he arranged to expose the killer

persons.name: Iris Marle
persons.notes: Rosemary's younger sister; the actual intended victim of the second poisoning

persons.name: Ruth Lessing
persons.notes: George Barton's secretary; co-conspirator with Victor Drake

persons.name: Victor Drake
persons.notes: Rosemary and Iris's cousin; would inherit Iris's fortune if she died

persons.name: Anthony Browne
persons.notes: One of Rosemary's admirers

persons.name: Stephen Farraday
persons.notes: Rosemary's admirer; attended both dinners

persons.name: Lady Sandra Farraday
persons.notes: Stephen Farraday's wife

deaths.cause_detail: Cyanide slipped into her champagne at a restaurant dinner at the Luxembourg; death initially ruled as suicide due to post-flu depression
deaths.motive_detail: Ruth Lessing and Victor Drake conspired to obtain Iris Marle's inheritance; with Rosemary dead, Iris was next
deaths.notes: Death ruled suicide but actually murder; the true target was eventually Iris, Rosemary's heir

deaths.cause_detail: Poisoned at the anniversary dinner he arranged to trap the murderer; the poisoned glass intended for Iris Marle was switched
deaths.motive_detail: George had been investigating Rosemary's death and was getting too close to the truth
deaths.notes: George set a trap for the killer but became the victim himself; the poisoned glass was meant for Iris

title: They Came to Baghdad
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Spy-adventure thriller inspired by Christie's own trips to Baghdad. Standalone novel.

persons.name: Victoria Jones
persons.notes: Bored secretary who follows a young man to Baghdad

persons.name: Henry Carmichael
persons.notes: British agent who passes critical information to Victoria before dying

persons.name: Edward
persons.notes: The young man Victoria followed to Baghdad; revealed as Lucifer, the mastermind villain

persons.name: Mr Dakin
persons.notes: Victoria's contact in British intelligence

persons.name: Dr Rathbone
persons.notes: Runs a cultural organisation; connected to the conspiracy

persons.name: Mrs Hamilton Clipp
persons.notes: Wealthy American who provides Victoria the opportunity to travel to Baghdad

persons.name: Richard Baker
persons.notes: The true romantic hero of the novel; staid archaeologist

deaths.cause_detail: Stabbed and found dying in Victoria's hotel room in Baghdad; his last words 'Lucifer...Basrah...Lefarge' become crucial clues
deaths.motive_detail: Carmichael was a British agent who had uncovered the conspiracy to sabotage the international summit; he was silenced
deaths.notes: Carmichael's dying words drive the investigation forward

title: Destination Unknown
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Also published as So Many Steps to Death in the US. Spy thriller featuring Hilary Craven and Mr Jessop.

persons.name: Hilary Craven
persons.notes: Deserted wife planning suicide who is recruited for a secret mission; impersonates Olive Betterton

persons.name: Mr Jessop
persons.notes: Recruits Hilary for the mission

persons.name: Thomas Betterton
persons.notes: Had murdered his first wife Elsa to steal credit for her scientific work; defected to Aristides's compound

persons.name: Olive Betterton
persons.notes: Thomas's second wife; dies from injuries in a plane crash before reaching Marrakesh

persons.name: Elsa Betterton
persons.notes: Thomas's first wife; the true scientific genius whose work Thomas stole; murdered by Thomas

persons.name: Mr Aristides
persons.notes: Built a secret compound to exploit missing scientists; arrested at the end

persons.name: Peters
persons.notes: Hilary's eventual romantic partner

deaths.cause_detail: Died from injuries sustained in a plane crash before landing in Marrakesh, while being transported to the secret compound
deaths.notes: Her death gives Hilary the opportunity to impersonate her and infiltrate the compound

deaths.cause_detail: Murdered by Thomas Betterton who then stole credit for her groundbreaking scientific work; backstory murder revealed during investigation
deaths.motive_detail: Thomas killed Elsa to take sole credit for her scientific discoveries and advance his own career
deaths.notes: Backstory murder; Thomas's inability to produce new work since her death exposes his fraud

title: Passenger to Frankfurt
creator: Agatha Christie
notes: Published to mark Christie's 80th birthday. Last of her spy novels. Subtitled 'An Extravaganza'.

persons.name: Sir Stafford Nye
persons.notes: Witty British diplomat who helps a mysterious woman at Frankfurt airport

persons.name: Daphne Theodofanous
persons.notes: Also known as Mary Ann; claims to be fleeing danger; recruits Nye

persons.name: Countess Charlotte von Waldsausen
persons.notes: Devises strategies for world domination; neo-Nazi conspirator

persons.name: Milly Jean Cortman
persons.notes: Wife of the American ambassador; revealed to be Juanita, an assassin in league with the neo-Nazis

persons.name: Lord Altamount
persons.notes: Target of an assassination attempt by poison

persons.name: Franz
persons.notes: Young man used as a figurehead for the neo-Nazi youth movement

persons.name: Great-Aunt Matilda
persons.notes: Stafford Nye's sharp elderly relative who provides insight

persons.name: Kleek
persons.notes: Attempts to poison Lord Altamount

deaths.cause_detail: Shot by Milly Jean Cortman (Juanita) when Kleek's attempt to poison him is blocked; Milly Jean shoots Lord Altamount herself
deaths.motive_detail: Part of a neo-Nazi conspiracy to destabilise world governments
deaths.notes: Milly Jean Cortman is revealed to be Juanita, an assassin; the murder underscores the global conspiracy
